title: In Imitation Of Dr. Swift : The Happy Life Of A Country Parson
content:
Parson, these things in thy possessing
Are better than the Bishop's blessing.
A Wife that makes conserves; a Steed
That carries double when there's need:
October store, and best Virginia,
Tithe-Pig, and mortuary Guinea:
Gazettes sent gratis down, and frank'd,
For which thy Patron's weekly thank'd;
A large Concordance, bound long since:
Sermons to Charles the First, when Prince;
A Chronicle of ancient standing;
A Chrysostom to smooth thy band in.
The Polygot � three parts, � my text,
Howbeit, � likewise � now to my next.
Lo here the Septuagint, � and Paul,
To sum the whole, � the close of all.
He that has these, may pass his life,
Drink with the 'Squire, and kiss his wife;
On Sundays preach, and eat his fill;
And fast on Fridays � if he will;
Toast Church and Queen, explain the News,
Talk with Church-Wardens about Pews,
Pray heartily for some new Gift,
And shake his head at Doctor S�t.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Alexander Pope delivers a powerful performance in "In Imitation Of Dr. Swift : The Happy Life Of A Country Parson"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
